page: Amazon Web Services m5d.2xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 Intel Xeon 8259CL processor operating at 3.1 GHz, configured with 8 vCPUs across 4 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The system is equipped with 32.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core.

A key feature of this instance is its bundled local storage, consisting of a 300 GB NVMe SSD instance store volume. This local storage provides low-latency, high-speed read and write capabilities directly attached to the host. Networking capabilities include a baseline bandwidth of 2 Gbps.

Benchmark results place the m5d.2xlarge in the average performance tier for multi-core CPU tasks, memory bandwidth, and database operations. Specifically, the instance achieves average ratings in stress-ng multi-core performance, Passmark CPU and memory scores, and Redis SET operations. However, single-core performance, including Geekbench single-core scores and single-threaded compression tasks, falls into the weaker bottom 25% tier. LLM inference capabilities are also limited, with prompt processing for medium models rated as poor.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the inclusion of local NVMe SSD storage within a general-purpose hardware profile offers a balanced resource density for workloads requiring temporary high-speed storage without the overhead of external volumes. This makes the instance suitable for general-purpose workloads, static web serving, software compilation, and database operations that can leverage the local instance store.
description: Amazon Web Services m5d.2xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor with 8 vCPUs, 4 physical cores, and 32.0 GB of DDR4 memory. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, it includes a bundled 300 GB NVMe SSD local instance store and a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps. Performance benchmarks indicate average capabilities for multi-core processing, memory bandwidth, and database operations, though single-core performance and LLM inference speeds are relatively weak. The qualitative cost efficiency of this instance is driven by its integrated local NVMe storage, which eliminates external storage overhead for temporary data. It is best suited for general-purpose applications, software compilation, static web serving, and database workloads that benefit from low-latency local storage.

tagline: A balanced general-purpose instance featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processing and integrated high-speed local NVMe SSD storage.

Bundled 300 GB NVMe SSD local instance store for low-latency storage
